title: Scalable dual-prefix hybrid memory-safe rerun

queries: candidate aware multiclass dual prefix memory safe rerun shared prefix raw label class id
autocomplete multiclass per sample shared prefix scoring memory safe dual view rerun

evidence.why_now: EXP111 was implementation-blocked by missing scorer initialization. EXP114 fixed that bug and showed the intended dual-prefix family is still resource-blocked on item-incoterms because the batched raw-label shared-prefix recursion exhausts GPU memory before the first evaluation. The next justified step is a memory-safe rerun of the same architecture family with per-sample prefix scoring rather than another scientific branch switch.

hypothesis: A memory-safe rerun that computes the dual-prefix shared-prefix scorer per sample should preserve the intended architecture while reducing peak KV-cache memory enough for item-incoterms to complete. This keeps the same candidate-aware dual-prefix family and tests it cleanly instead of confounding the result with a different architecture.
notes: This is a clean rerun of the same dual-prefix architecture after converting the shared-prefix scorer to a memory-safe per-sample execution path.
Do not mix any new loss, sampling, packaging, or pretrain changes into this rerun.
Launched on 2026-05-08 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Resolved idle-GPU placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPU 0, user-ltv -> lab25211 GPU 5, item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPU 6.
Follow-up orchestrator status check after launch showed all three windows up and logs in DB-load startup.
Completed on 2026-05-08 as a resource-blocked bundle.
user-churn and user-ltv finished, but item-incoterms still ran out of memory before the first evaluation even after moving the shared-prefix scorer to a per-sample path.
Treat this as evidence that the dual-prefix family now needs an explicit multi-GPU fallback rather than another single-GPU rerun.
